chunks.page_summary: This page provides instructions for establishing USB communication with a Rheonics SME device using the Rheonics Control Panel software.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Network Setup through USB communication for Rheonics SME
Modified on: Thu, 25 May, 2023 at 12:46 AM

1. Establishing USB Communication with the SME

1.1. Start the RCP software and go to the "Settings" tab.

Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.6
Tabs: Measurement, Events, Settings, Service, Diagnostics, Communication, Temp. Control, Logger, Help

Measured Values
Viscosity: not connected
Density: not connected
Temperature: not connected
Pressure: not connected

Status
Error: not connected E99
Logging: Stopped
Pressure: Select Channel
Manual entry
1 bar

Log Measurements
Sample Name
Start Logging
Add Event

Sensor Information
Serial Number: B11-0103
Fres: -
df: -

Graph Labels:
Viscosity [mPa.s]
Density [g/cc]
Temperature [°C]
Pressure [bar]
hh:mm:ss

Figure 1. Starting the RCP software.

1.2. Click the "Autodetect" button to connect automatically with the Rheonics sensor.
key_facts: The document provides instructions for network setup through USB communication for Rheonics SME.
The document was modified on Thursday, May 25, 2023, at 12:46 AM.
The first step is to start the Rheonics Control Panel (RCP) software, version 5.4.0.6, and navigate to the 'Settings' tab.
The RCP software displays measured values for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure.
The software includes a status section indicating 'Error: not connected' (E99) and 'Logging: Stopped'.
A 'Log Measurements' section allows for entering a sample name and controlling logging.
Sensor information, including Serial Number 'B11-0103', Fres, and df, is displayed.
The second step involves clicking an 'Autodetect' button to automatically connect with the Rheonics sensor.
The software features graphs for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure over time (hh:mm:ss).

chunks.page_summary: This page illustrates the steps for establishing communication and configuring settings within the Rheonics Control Panel software, specifically highlighting the autodetect function and various measurement parameters.

key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software (version 5.4.0.6) is used to manage and configure Rheonics devices.
The 'Settings' tab in the software allows users to configure communication, sensor, unit, resolution, pressure gauge, graph, and log settings.
The 'Autodetect' function can automatically identify the correct communication port.
Communication can be established via USB, Ethernet, or Bluetooth.
A red LED indicates that communication is not yet established, while a green LED signifies successful communication.
Upon successful communication, the software automatically selects the correct port.
The software provides options for setting units for viscosity (mPa.s, cP, cSt), density (g/cc, kg/m³, lb/gal, lb/ft³), temperature (°C, °K, °F), and pressure (bar, kPa, MPa, psi, torr, atm).
Graph settings include auto or fixed scales for viscosity, density, temperature, and pressure, with default min/max values.
Logging settings allow configuration of logging intervals, measurement frequency, and debug information logging.
The software allows plotting damping (df) and resonant frequency (Fres) instead of Viscosity & Density in the graph.
The USB port is utilized by the SME (Subject Matter Expert).
diagram_labels: Figure 2. Settings Tab-Autodetect Function is activated.
USB port used by the SME
Figure 3. Communication through serial port initiated.

chunks.page_summary: This page provides step-by-step instructions for configuring a static IP address on an SME device using the Rheonics Control Panel software, including details on Ethernet parameters, Modbus settings, and operational history.
chunks.extracted_text:
2. Configuring the static IP address on the SME
2.1. Go to the "Communication" tab and next on the Ethernet settings box change the DHCP parameter to "False" so the network settings can be edited.

Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.6
Measurement | Events | Settings | Service | Diagnostics | Communication | Temp. Control | Logger | Help

Modbus RTU (RS-485)
Modbus Address: 1
Baud Rate: 38400
Parity: ODD

LCD Display (SME-TRD Only)
Parameter:
Line 1: Density, Resolution: 0.0000
Line 2: Viscosity, Resolution: 0.00
Line 3: Temperature, Resolution: 0.00

Write and Update
Write Config
Load Config

Ethernet
Ethernet MAC: 70:B3:D5:D2:03:A2
DHCP: True (Set DHCP to False indicated by arrow)
IP: 0.0.0.0
Subnet: 0.0.0.0
Gateway IP: 0.0.0.0
DNS: 0.0.0.0
Terminal Port: 10001

Location ID
Location ID: 
Location Name: 
Latitude: 0.000000
Longitude: 0.000000
Altitude: 0

Operation History
RTC Time: 24/05/23 17:09:03
Last power Reset: 04/05/23 21:03:27
Supply Voltage: 23.79 V
SME Temp.: 35.10 °C
Max. Sensor Temp. Exposure: 643.43 °C
Max. SME Temp. Exposure: 43.26 °C

Bluetooth LTE 4.0
MAC: 

Figure 4. Communication tab in RCP

2.2. Fill Ethernet parameters based on your Host pc network parameters.

Ethernet
Ethernet MAC: 70:B3:D5:D2:03:A2
DHCP: False
IP: 192.168.3.18
Subnet: 255.255.255.0
Gateway IP: 0.0.0.0
DNS: 0.0.0.0
Terminal Port: 10001

Figure 5. Ethernet Parameters
key_facts: To configure a static IP address on the SME, the DHCP parameter in the Ethernet settings must be changed to "False".
The Rheonics Control Panel version shown is 5.4.0.6.
Ethernet parameters, including IP, Subnet, Gateway IP, and DNS, should be configured based on the Host PC network settings.
The default Modbus Address is 1, Baud Rate is 38400, and Parity is ODD.
The LCD Display can be configured to show Density, Viscosity, and Temperature.
Operation history provides real-time clock, last power reset time, supply voltage, SME temperature, and maximum sensor/SME temperature exposure.
The Ethernet MAC address for the device is 70:B3:D5:D2:03:A2.

chunks.page_summary: This page displays the Rheonics Control Panel application interface (version 5.4.0.6), detailing various configuration and operational parameters for a device, and provides instructions for saving settings and configuring network parameters.
chunks.extracted_text:
2.3. Click on the “Write config” button and wait until it lights green.

Rheonics Control Panel 5.4.0.6

  Max. Sensor Temp. Exposure: 643.43 °C

Figure 6. Uploading parameters

2.4. Input the IP address (Using the same IP address used in the step before) and select the Ethernet option, click the “Apply” button. If everything is correct status led should be green.
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel application is at version 5.4.0.6.

The control panel allows configuration of Modbus RTU (RS-485) communication parameters, LCD display settings for parameters like Density, Viscosity, and Temperature, Ethernet network parameters, and location identification details.
Operational history records include Real-Time Clock (RTC), last power reset timestamp, current supply voltage, SME temperature, and maximum exposure temperatures for both the sensor and the SME.
To save configurations, the 'Write Config' button must be clicked and should light green upon success.
When inputting an IP address and applying Ethernet settings, the status LED should turn green if the configuration is correct.

3. References
1. Verifying the network setup on the PC where the RCP software is installed
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000397449-verifying-the-network-setup-on-the-pc-where-the-rcp-software-is-installed)
key_facts: The Rheonics Control Panel software version shown is 5.4.0.6.
The software interface allows configuration of operator details, communication settings, sensor type, measurement units, logging parameters, and graph scales.
Ethernet communication is selected, indicating the system can connect via network, with an example IP address of 192.168.3.18 and port 10001.
A green status indicator confirms that communication is successfully established.
The default sensor type is SRD, and units are pre-selected as mPa.s for viscosity, g/cc for density, °C for temperature, and bar for pressure.
Logging is configured with a 5-second interval and a 3-second measurement frequency.
Graph settings provide auto-scaling options or fixed ranges for viscosity (1-30 mPa.s), density (0-1.5 g/cc), temperature (10-60 °C), and pressure (1-10 bar).
The document includes a reference to an article on verifying network setup for the RCP software installation.
warnings_notes: A reference is provided for verifying the network setup on the PC where the RCP software is installed, which can be found at https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000397449-verifying-the-network-setup-on-the-pc-where-the-rcp-software-is-installed.
